steps.title: Avoid a comma splice
steps.content: The main clause says NATO members share a commitment to collective defense. After the comma, the sentence needs a nonfinite phrase explaining that commitment, not another independent clause.

steps.title: Use the participle
steps.content: A, C, and D are finite verb forms, so each would create an independent clause joined to the first with only a comma. The present participle creates a phrase that attaches to the main clause. The answer is B.
